Chorley Transparency Scorecard: How Open Is Your Council?

Key Findings

  • Chorley Borough Council's published spending data covers a total of £372.2M across five financial years.
  • The council recorded 21,421 transactions during the period from 2021/22 to 2025/26.
  • Spending decreased by £29.6M, representing a 52.7% reduction, from 2021 to 2025.
  • A significant year-on-year increase of £67.2M, or 119.5%, was observed between 2021 and 2022.
  • Conversely, spending saw a sharp drop of £-150,973,076, or -95.2%, from 2023 to 2024.

Unpacking Chorley Borough Council's Spending Data

Chorley Borough Council, a district council in Lancashire, England, has made public its spending records covering five financial years. This commitment to transparency, guided by the Local Government Transparency Code, allows residents to scrutinise how public money is allocated. AI DOGE's analysis of this data reveals a comprehensive overview of the council's financial activities from 2021/22 to 2025/26. Over this period, the council processed a substantial volume of transactions, totalling 21,421 individual payments.

The cumulative spending analysed amounts to £372.2M. This figure represents all transactions published by the council within the specified timeframe. Furthermore, the data indicates that the council engaged with 2,175 unique suppliers. Understanding the breadth of suppliers and the total financial outlay provides a foundational insight into the council's operational scale and its economic footprint within the community.

Analysing Spending Fluctuations Across Five Years

A detailed examination of Chorley Borough Council's spending reveals significant year-on-year fluctuations. The data shows a dynamic financial landscape, with periods of both substantial growth and sharp contraction. From 2021 to 2025, the overall spending decreased by £29.6M, a reduction of 52.7%. This overall trend, however, masks considerable volatility within the intervening years.

The most notable increase occurred between 2021 and 2022, when spending surged from £56.2M to £123.4M. This represents an increase of £67.2M, or 119.5%. Following this, spending continued to rise from 2022 to 2023, reaching £158.5M, an increase of £35.2M or 28.5%. The peak spending year within this period was 2023. However, the subsequent year, 2024, saw a dramatic decrease. Spending plummeted to just £7.6M, marking a reduction of £-150,973,076, or -95.2%, from the previous year. This sharp decline is a critical point for public scrutiny. By 2025, spending had recovered somewhat to £26.6M, an increase of £19.0M or 252.0% from the low point in 2024.

To provide a clearer picture of these shifts, the year-over-year spending figures are presented below:

Year Spending Change from Previous Year (£) Change from Previous Year (%)
2021 £56.2M - -
2022 £123.4M +£67.2M +119.5%
2023 £158.5M +£35.2M +28.5%
2024 £7.6M £-150,973,076 -95.2%
2025 £26.6M +£19.0M +252.0%

Implications for Public Oversight and Accountability

The detailed spending data from Chorley Borough Council offers a valuable resource for public oversight. The publication of 21,421 transactions over five years demonstrates a commitment to the principles of the Local Government Transparency Code. However, the significant fluctuations in spending, particularly the sharp drop of -95.2% from 2023 to 2024, warrant further investigation. Understanding the underlying reasons for such dramatic shifts is crucial for residents to assess the council's financial management and strategic priorities.

The presence of 2,175 unique suppliers suggests a diverse range of services and goods procured by the council. While the raw data provides the figures, deeper analysis is often needed to understand the nature of these expenditures and whether they represent value for money. Transparency platforms like AI DOGE aim to make this complex data more accessible, empowering citizens to engage with their local council's spending decisions. The availability of five years of data, from 2021/22 to 2025/26, allows for a longer-term perspective on financial trends and accountability.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Chorley Borough Council about the reasons behind the -95.2% spending decrease from 2023 to 2024.
  • Attend council committee meetings, as they are open to the public, to hear discussions on budget and financial planning.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to understand the nature and value of agreements with its 2,175 unique suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor about concerns regarding the significant year-on-year spending fluctuations and their impact on local services.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Chorley Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Chorley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
